per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA states a storage-and-deletion retention rule, while the REG states conditions under which the data subject may process personal data except storage; these do not clearly express the same or directly conflicting constraint.

The REA sets a retention practice ('store ... for 7 days and then delete it'), while the REG requires the controller to provide information about the storage period; this is not a direct conflict and omission of the information-provision duty is not a deviation.

The REA specifies how long data is stored and when it is deleted, while the REG requires the controller to inform the data subject of the storage period; these are different constraints and omission alone is not a deviation.
